subject: midwest energy hub llc - name change to enovate energy

emails.subject: Re: MidWest Energy Hub LLC - Name Change to Enovate Energy
emails.subject_base: MidWest Energy Hub LLC - Name Change to Enovate Energy
emails.body: Matt Per our conversation today, the following e-mail was sent by Russell Murrell regarding the impending name change of Midwest Energy Hub, L.L.C. My understanding is that we have already bought/sold natural gas in Oklahoma, Texas, and Louisianna. The remaining states in his e-mail are potential future places of business. I will leave it up to your discretion as to how you want to pursue registering for taxes in these locales. I am guessing we may want to register in those states we have already transacted in. Please let me know what you think we should do. Thanks, Gregg Forwarded by Gregg Penman/Corp/Enron on 08/21/2000 02:33 PM Russell E Murrell 08/21/2000 02:19 PM

emails.subject: Re: MidWest Energy Hub LLC - Name Change to Enovate Energy
emails.subject_base: MidWest Energy Hub LLC - Name Change to Enovate Energy
emails.body: Gregg: We should register Midwest Energy Hub, L.L.C. ("Midwest") for Sales Use Tax in those states in which Midwest has nexus. Nexus requires a physical presence (employees or inventory). If Midwest, in foreign states (outside of Illinois), is merely purchasing and selling natural gas, for delivery by common carrier; Midwest does not have nexus. Rather, Midwest is acting much like a mail order seller. In light of the above, could you please advise regarding Midwest's physical presence in Iowa, Indiana, Kansas, Louisiana, Michigan, Oklahoma, Texas and Wisconsin. Thanks. Matt x33979 From: Gregg Penman ENRON 08/21/2000 04:37 PM

emails.subject: Re: MidWest Energy Hub LLC - Name Change to Enovate Energy
emails.subject_base: MidWest Energy Hub LLC - Name Change to Enovate Energy
emails.body: Matt Thanks for the explanation. That makes life much simpler, at least for now. Illinois is currently the only state where we have employees or inventory. We are merely purchasing and selling natural gas in the other locations. If that changes in the future, I will let you know. As always thanks for your thorough review, Gregg
